summary.intent: Fix and resolve all open review comments on PR #444 in the moltis-org/moltis repository, specifically addressing feedback about tool name sanitization logic.
summary.outcome: Two new greptile review comments were addressed: an invariant comment for suffix stripping was added and a test for the edge case of 'functions_' alone (yielding empty string) was added. All 15 tests passed, formatting was clean, and changes were committed.

repo: Tool name sanitization uses single-pass suffix stripping intentionally to preserve legitimate names like 'my_mcp_3000' — double stripping was explicitly declined.
The codebase follows an INVARIANT comment convention for documenting assumptions about registered tool names.
Hook dispatch sites derive tc_name from sanitized.to_string() rather than tc.name.clone() to ensure hooks receive sanitized names.

code.finding: INVARIANT comment asserting no registered tool name starts with 'functions_' lives here.
First hook dispatch site — uses sanitized name (tc_name from sanitized.to_string()).
Second hook dispatch site — also uses sanitized name.

workflow: PR review comments were fetched via gh CLI to identify resolved vs. new threads before making any code changes.
After edits, both cargo test (targeted to sanitize_tool_name) and rustfmt check were run before committing.
Greptile is an active automated reviewer on this repo and posts follow-up comments between sessions.

friction: Required multiple reads of runner.rs to locate the correct line ranges for edits due to the file's size.
Had to distinguish between already-resolved comments from a prior session and new comments from the current session to avoid duplicate work.
open_items: The deliberate decision not to implement double suffix stripping may warrant a comment explaining the rationale in the sanitize_tool_name function for future contributors.
Edge case of sanitize_tool_name producing an empty string (input 'functions_') is now tested but the caller behavior when an empty name results is not explicitly validated.
